exact_statement.name: R1b F2-linear combination no-new-long-chord reduction
exact_statement.formal_surface: For every repository-native admissible finite-patch FGL instance I, long-chord edge e_i, finite family of trivial 2-face boundaries b_j = partial tau_j, and F2 coefficients a_j, if e_i is absent from supp(b_j) for every j, then e_i is absent from supp(sum_j a_j b_j).
quantifiers: forall long_chord_edge e_i with i in {1,2}
forall finite family of trivial 2-face boundaries b_j = partial tau_j
hypotheses: for every j, e_i notin supp(b_j)
sum_j a_j b_j is computed over F2
exact_statement.algebraic_core: xor_j (a_j AND indicator(e_i in supp(b_j))) = 0 whenever every indicator(e_i in supp(b_j)) = 0
exact_statement.r1_dependency_role: This discharges the algebraic cancellation part of R1b once R1a supplies generator-level absence for each trivial 2-face boundary. It does not prove the geometric generator-level absence itself.

proof_certificate.edge_coordinate_model: Each boundary is evaluated at the coordinate e_i as a bit in {0,1}.
proof_certificate.generator_absence_assumption: for every generator b_j, indicator(e_i in supp(b_j)) = 0
proof_certificate.coefficient_rule: each F2 coefficient a_j is a bit in {0,1}
proof_certificate.sum_coordinate_rule: indicator(e_i in supp(sum_j a_j b_j)) = xor_j (a_j AND indicator(e_i in supp(b_j)))
derivation: By generator_absence_assumption, every indicator(e_i in supp(b_j)) is 0.
For every coefficient a_j in F2, a_j AND 0 = 0.
The xor of a finite family of zeros is 0.
Therefore indicator(e_i in supp(sum_j a_j b_j)) = 0.
Therefore e_i is absent from the support of every F2-linear combination of such generators.

discharged_subclaim.name: R1b algebraic closure from R1a generator absence
discharged_subclaim.logical_form: If every trivial 2-face boundary excludes e_i, then every F2-linear combination of trivial 2-face boundaries excludes e_i.
conditional_on: R1a generator-level absence for each trivial 2-face boundary
repository-native definition that W^triv is generated over F2 by trivial 2-face boundaries

remaining_for_r1.r1a: OPEN: prove every trivial 2-face boundary excludes both long-chord edges.
remaining_for_r1.r1c: OPEN: prove maximal-separation geometry forbids any trivial witness boundary from traversing a diameter-spanning long chord.
remaining_for_r1.generation_bridge: OPEN generation bridge unless already present in the repo: prove W^triv is exactly the F2 span of trivial 2-face boundaries used by R1a/R1b.

required_non_claims: does not prove combined R1 Long-Chord Exclusion Lemma
does not prove P vs NP
does not prove any Clay problem
